name: Classic Masala Chai
steps: Crush cardamom pods and grate ginger
Boil water with ginger and cardamom for 2 minutes
Add tea leaves and simmer for 1 minute
Pour in Dodla Toned Milk and bring to boil
Add sugar, strain and serve hot

name: Paneer Bhurji
steps: Heat oil, add cumin seeds until they splutter
Sauté onions until golden brown
Add tomatoes and green chilli, cook until mushy
Add turmeric, salt and mix well
Crumble in Dodla Paneer and toss on high heat for 3 minutes
Garnish with fresh coriander and serve with rotis

name: Mango Lassi
steps: Blend mango pieces until smooth puree
Add Dodla Curd, sugar and cardamom
Blend again until frothy
Serve chilled with ice cubes
Garnish with saffron strands

name: Ghee Rice (Nei Sadam)
steps: Wash and soak rice for 20 minutes
Heat Dodla Ghee in a heavy pot
Fry cashews and raisins until golden, set aside
Sauté onions with whole spices until caramelised
Add drained rice, toast for 2 minutes
Add 3.5 cups water and salt, cook covered on low heat 18 minutes
Fluff with fork and top with fried cashews and raisins

name: Shrikhand
steps: Hang Dodla Curd in a muslin cloth for 3-4 hours to drain whey
Transfer hung curd to a bowl
Dissolve saffron in 1 tsp warm milk
Beat curd with sugar until smooth and creamy
Mix in cardamom and saffron milk
Refrigerate for 1 hour and serve chilled
Garnish with pistachios

name: Butter Naan (Tandoor Style)
steps: Dissolve yeast and sugar in warm Dodla Milk, rest 10 min
Mix flour and salt, add yeast mixture and knead 10 minutes
Rest dough covered for 30 minutes until doubled
Roll into oval shapes ½cm thick
Cook on a very hot griddle 2 min per side until charred spots appear
Brush generously with Dodla Butter while hot
Top with garlic and coriander
